issues.comment: The parameterless `canManage()` checks for both VIEW and MANAGE scopes via `hasPermission(null, AdminPermissionsSchema.VIEW, AdminPermissionsSchema.MANAGE)`, but it should only check for MANAGE scope. This allows users with only `view` permission on groups to pass `canManage()`, which gates operations like `addTopLevelGroup()`. This is a privilege escalation — view-only users can create and modify groups.

`getGroupIdsWithViewPermission()` uses `groupResource.getId()` (the authorization Resource entity's internal UUID) instead of `groupResource.getName()` (which stores the actual group ID). This causes two problems: (1) `hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...)` passes the wrong identifier, causing it to check the wrong resource; (2) `granted.add(groupResource.getId())` adds resource entity IDs instead of actual group IDs, so the set used to filter user search by group membership will never match any real groups, causing user searches to return no results.
